descriptionLines: Check if the devices have received multicast traffic for an entry within specificed
`multicast_groups` (if group range is given, just need at least one group found within given range).
The check uses the output of the `show ip mroute summary vrf all`
Logic:
- Create list of possible groups in target range (if no prefix length is specified, assumed
/32 and only one IP is checked)
- Loop through possible IPs looking for a match of VRF and IP in multicast table
- If a possible group IP is found during loop, mark entire range validated (traffic present)
- If no possible group is found during the loop, mark entire range not validated (no traffic
present)

Multicast group range 239.255.128.0/17 has been found active in VRF default
descriptionLines: Check if the devices have received multicast traffic for an entry within specificed
`multicast_groups` (if group range is given, just need at least one group found within given range).
The check uses the output of the `show ip mroute summary vrf all`
Logic:
- Create list of possible groups in target range (if no prefix length is specified, assumed
/32 and only one IP is checked)
- Loop through possible IPs looking for a match of VRF and IP in multicast table
- If a possible group IP is found during loop, mark entire range validated (traffic present)
- If no possible group is found during the loop, mark entire range not validated (no traffic
present)
examples.commandHint: show ip mroute summary vrf all | xml

Command: show ip mroute summary vrf all | xml
